---
title: "FARA"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
library(DT)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)
library(leaflet)
```

```{r}
fara <- fara %>% 
  clean_names()

```

```{r}
#I am now going to make this data set only last from 2012-2024
fara$date <- as.Date(fara$date_stamped, format="%m/%d/%Y")
fara$year <- as.numeric(format(fara$date, "%Y"))

# Filter dates between 2012 and 2024
filtered_fara <- subset(fara, format(date, "%Y") >= 2012 & format(date, "%Y") <= 2024)

print(filtered_fara)
write.csv(filtered_fara, "~/Downloads/filtered_fara.csv",row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
pre_2008 <- fara[fara$year <= 2007, ]
unique_pre_2008 <- unique(pre_2008$registration_number)
```

```{r}
all_previous_regs <- unique_pre_2008
years <- sort(unique(fara$year))
new_regs_by_year <- list()

for (yr in years[years > 2007]) {
  year_data <- fara[fara$year == yr, ]
  year_regs <- unique(year_data$registration_number)
  new_regs <- setdiff(year_regs, all_previous_regs)
  new_regs_by_year[[as.character(yr)]] <- new_regs
  all_previous_regs <- union(all_previous_regs, new_regs)
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Create a summary data frame for plotting
year_counts <- data.frame(
  year = as.integer(names(new_regs_by_year)),
  new_registrations = sapply(new_regs_by_year, length)
)

# Plot
ggplot(year_counts, aes(x = year, y = new_registrations)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "New Unique Registration Numbers by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of New Registrations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
year_counts <- data.frame(
  year = as.integer(names(new_regs_by_year)),
  new_registrations = sapply(new_regs_by_year, length)
)
```

```{r}
write.csv(year_counts, "new_unique_registrations_by_year.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
